result.markdown:
---
title: Mapping Celestia's Network
---

*"The Lord has been and is very kind to me in giving me so many friends may I ever be grateful for all his benefits."*

**Celestia Webster's Diary - May 17, 1867**

## C's Network


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(sf)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}

```
Linking to GEOS 3.13.0, GDAL 3.8.5, PROJ 9.5.1; sf_use_s2() is TRUE
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
library(tidygeocoder)
library(USAboundaries)
library(tidyverse)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}

```
── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
✔ dplyr     1.2.0     ✔ readr     2.2.0
✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
✔ ggplot2   4.0.2     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
✔ lubridate 1.9.5     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
✔ purrr     1.2.1     
```


:::

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}

```
── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
cnetwork <- read.csv("C_Network.csv")

locations <- geo(c("Conesus", "Livonia", "Springwater", "Hemlock"))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}

```
Passing 4 addresses to the Nominatim single address geocoder
Query completed in: 4 seconds
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
locations
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 4 × 3
  address       lat  long
  <chr>       <dbl> <dbl>
1 Conesus      42.7 -77.7
2 Livonia      42.4 -83.4
3 Springwater  44.5 -79.8
4 Hemlock      43.4 -84.2
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
locations_sf <- st_as_sf(cnetwork, coords = c("lng", "lat"), crs = 4326)
locations_sf
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```

Dimension:     XY

Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84

9                    D.T. Campbell POINT (-77.66914 42.70185)
10                        T Powell POINT (-77.63345 42.72366)
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
newyork <- us_counties(states = "New York")

# Find EPSG code for New York
state_plane(state = "NY")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] 32116
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
# Project
locs_proj <- st_transform(locations_sf, "EPSG:32146")
newyork_proj <- st_transform(newyork, "EPSG:32116")

ggplot() + 
  geom_sf(data = newyork_proj) + 
  geom_sf(data = locs_proj) + 
  labs(title = "Celestia's Network in Livingston County")
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Map_C_Network_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-1-1.png){width=672}
:::
